html:
Exclusive to:

StageMocking Bird

“A gift graciously bestowed to you by the Queen of the Constant, to enrich your sad, futile little lives. She was even kind enough to prepare costumes and a script!”

-

The Stage is a naturally generating Structure in Don't Starve Together. It always spawns in a Set Piece consisting of tiles of Lush Carpet and Mosaic Flooring, with a Lectern and two Prickly Stagehands beside it. The Set Piece often spawns in Triple MacTusk biome, although it can also spawn elsewhere.

The Stage can be used to act out a few plays. The player can insert a Playbill into the Lectern to change the play. One of the plays is written by Charlie and serves as a metaphor of her life and how she came to rule over The Constant from the Nightmare Throne.

Players can interact with the Stage to begin the play, but they must have the correct Costumes and number of characters. Examining the Lectern shows the required characters for the next act.

If there isn't enough players to fulfill the needs for the entire cast, Mannequins wearing the costumes can substitute for them. Excess Mannequins are ignored, and they must be built on the Stage to count as performers: the Set Piece comes with a single Mannequin off-stage, which holds a complete Doll costume to start the players off.

Contents

    • 1.1 Poor Performance
    • 1.2 Bad costumes
    • 1.3 Repeat costumes
    • 1.4 No script

The Plays

When a play begins, Mocking Birds will appear, perching on top of the Stage to spectate and provide commentary, heckling the actors in the process. Shadow hands will possess the involved actors and have them act out the scenes. If the casting is incorrect or the script is interrupted, the performance fails, spawning 3-6 Briar Wolves and closing the Stage for 3 – 6 days.

Players can also perform a soliloquy if they are the sole actor, with unique soliloquies for both the characters in the play and the survivors themselves. There is also an epilogue scene featuring the Blacksmith and Queen.

Poor Performance

Bad costumes

Mocking Birds

  • "Something's wrong!",
  • "Check your costumes!",
  • "We'll come back.",
  • "When you get it right!",

Repeat costumes

Mocking Birds

  • "Are you kidding?"
  • "Am I seeing double?!"
  • "No understudies!"

No script

Mocking Birds

  • "Something's off."
  • "They're going off-script!"
  • "I didn't come for improv!"
  • "We're out."

Character Specific

Wilson soliloquy

  • "I am the very model of a modern Major-General"
  • "I've information vegetable, animal, and mineral"
  • "I'm very well acquainted, too, with matters Mathematical"
  • "I understand equations, both the simple and quadratical"

Willow soliloquy

  • "Fire fire, burning bright,"
  • "In the forests of the night;"
  • "And in the mountains, and the plains,"
  • "And the towns, and the cities,"
  • "Everywhere! Burn it all down!! Hahaha!!"
  • (Willow performs the "/happy" emote.)
  • "Yeah, pretty sure that's how that poem goes."

Wolfgang soliloquy

  • "Wolfgang is not just man of big muscles,"
  • "Is man of mighty feelings too."
  • "Will read poem to you now:"
  • "Wolfgang likes his friends,"
  • "Though some are weird, make scary magic, and one is spider,"
  • "Will stay with them till very end,"
  • "Make Wolfgang's bad day brighter."

Wendy soliloquy

  • "O what can ail thee, knight-at-arms,"
  • "So haggard and so woe-begone?"
  • "The squirrel's granary is full,"
  • "And the harvest's done."
  • "I see a lily on thy brow,"
  • "With anguish moist and fever-dew,"
  • "And on thy cheeks a fading rose"
  • "Fast withereth too."

WX-78 soliloquy

  • "PERCEIVE ME, FLESHLINGS"
  • "YES. GOOD"
  • "I KNOW YOU MEATBAGS ARE WEAK-WILLED"
  • "SO AS YOUR OVERLORD"
  • "I HAVE DECIDED TO ENGAGE IN..."
  • "UGH..."
  • "\"MOTIVATIONAL SPEAKING\""
  • "TO INSPIRE YOU TO BE MORE USEFUL TO ME"
  • "BUT SINCE YOU HAVE SAD LITTLE MEAT BRAINS"
  • "I AM KEEPING MY EXPECTATIONS LOW"
  • "IS THE INSPIRATION WORKING YET?"

Wickerbottom soliloquy

  • "Theater as an art form is practiced throughout the world."
  • "The Western tradition of theater can trace its origins back to ancient Athens;"
  • "the word "theater" of course coming from the Greek "theatron,""
  • "which itself was derived from the verb "theasthai," "to behold.""
  • "Isn't etymology delightful?"

Woodie soliloquy

Woodie

  • "Er... I don't really like bein' the center of attention."

Lucy (if in inventory)

  • "Let me try!"
  • "Chop chop chop, swing swing swing,"
  • "Choppin' is my favourite thing."
  • "Swing swing swing, chop chop chop,"
  • "Love it way too much to stop!"

Woodie

  • "You've sure got a way with words, Lucy."

Wes soliloquy (Only exists in game files)

  • "Essayez-vous comprendre mes pensées?"
  • "Ah, comme c'est gentil à vous!"
  • "Je suis flatté. :)"

Directly translated from french, it reads:

  • "Are you trying to understand my thoughts?"
  • "Ah, that's very kind of you!"
  • "I am flattered. :)"

Maxwell soliloquy

  • "Thank you very much, you are too kind."
  • "And now, I will pull shadows incarnate from this mysterious tome!"
  • Maxwell summons two temporary shadow minions that dance next to him before disappearing for his next line.
  • "The show was better with... well, it was better before."

Wigfrid soliloquy

  • (Wigfrid sings opera for several seconds.)

Webber soliloquy ("boy")

  • "Ahem! We're going to say a poem now!"
  • "Little Miss Muffet she sat on her tuffet,"
  • "eating her curds and whey."
  • "Along came a spider who sat down beside her"
  • "And frightened Miss Muffet away!"

Webber soliloquy ("spider")

  • ""Will you walk into my parlour?""
  • "said a spider to a fly;"
  • "'Tis the prettiest little parlour that ever you did spy."
  • "The way into my parlour is up a winding stair,"
  • "And I have many pretty things to shew when you are there."
  • ""Oh no, no!" said the little fly"
  • "to ask me is in vain"
  • "For who goes up your winding stair can ne'er come down again."

Winona soliloquy

  • "There once was a gal from the sticks,"
  • "Who went out to look for her sis."
  • "But she found her too late,"
  • "And got yanked through a gate,"
  • "Ain't family drama the pits?"

Warly soliloquy

  • "Adieu, patrie!"
  • "L'onde est en furie."
  • "Adieu, patrie,"
  • "Azur!"
  • "Adieu, maison, treille au fruit mûr,"
  • "Adieu, les fleurs d'or du vieux mur!"

Wortox soliloquy

  • "The wisest aunt, telling the saddest tale,"
  • "Sometime for three-foot stool mistaketh me;"
  • "Then slip I from her bum, down topples she,"
  • "And 'tailor' cries, and falls into a cough;"
  • "And then the whole quire hold their hips and laugh,"
  • "And waxen in their mirth and neeze and swear"
  • "A merrier hour was never wasted there."

Wormwood soliloquy

  • "Hello friends!"
  • "Watching? Hmm..."
  • "Doo-doo-dee-doop"
  • "Woo-wah-wah!"
  • "Deedle-deedle-deedle-woo"
  • "Wah-wah-wheee!"
  • "Oop-doodle-doo"
  • "Bwa-waaaaaaaaaah!"

Wurt soliloquy

  • "Glurp... th-this is fairy story. Made by Wurt."
  • "Once upon time, there was super strong Mermfolk princess."
  • "Merm princess build houses for all her friends,"
  • "and make pretty decorations from bones of her enemies."
  • "Everyone live happily ever after, the end."

Walter soliloquy

  • "I've been teaching Woby lots of tricks, watch!"
  • "Okay Woby... stay!"
  • "Good girl!"
  • "We're uh... we're still working on the rest."
  • "Still pretty impressive though, right?"

Wanda soliloquy

  • ""You are old, Father William," the young man said",
  • ""And your hair has become very white;",
  • "And yet you incessantly stand on your head—",
  • "Do you think, at your age, it is right?"",
  • ""In my youth," Father William replied to his son,",
  • ""I feared it might injure the brain;",
  • "But now that I'm perfectly sure I have none,",
  • "Why, I do it again and again."",

Year of the Clockwork Knight

Only during the Event: Year of the Clockwork Knight

During the event Year of the Clockwork Knight, some of the stage structures will look different. Also after the player makes an offer to the Clockwork Knight Shrine, coming close to the Stage will spawn 2 Briar Wolves that drop Red Pouch, a Briar Wolf that drops Princess Mask, a Briar Wolf that drops Princess Costume and, if the player does not have My Knights Four and I, a Briar Wolf that drops My Knights Four and I.

Trivia

  • Wilson quotes the song "Major-General's Song" from Gilbert and Sullivan's 1879 comic opera The Pirates of Penzance.
  • Willow quotes her own modified version of The Tyger by William Blake.
  • Wendy quotes the 1819 poem "La Belle Dame sans Merci" (or in English, "the Beautiful Lady Without Mercy") by John Keats, with a title derived from an earlier 15th century work by Alain Chartier, whose works are alluded to in the 1819 poem.
  • Woodie's soliloquy will be cut short after just the first line if Lucy is not in his inventory, regardless if she's on-stage with him.
    • Her text is visible to survivors other than the Woodie holding her as she "performs".
  • Maxwell's commentary in the end of the Act 2 Scene 1 was "Is that really how she remembers it?".
  • Wigfrid sings instead of having a soliloquy, making her the only character with in-game voice acting (as opposed to an instrument).
    • Despite this, her soliloquy is ironically the only one to use zero actual words.
  • Webber quotes either the nursery rhyme "Little Miss Muffet" or the poem "The Spider and the Fly" at random.
    • This also makes him the only character with multiple, wholly distinct soliloquys to himself.
  • Warly quotes "Le chant de ceux qui s'en vont sur mer" (or in English The Seaman's Song) by Victor Hugo, circa 1868.
  • Wortox quotes A Midsummer Night's Dream, specifically Act 2 Scene 1.
  • Wanda quotes the poem "You Are Old, Father William" from Alice's Adventures in Wonderland.
  • In a Refresh Rhymes With Play Dev Cast about "A Little Drama Update", May Wa came in with homemade Mocking Birds puppets. Later, she posted via Instagram instructions on how to create such puppets.
  • While standing on the stage, the player has a unique talking animation, gesturing about as they talk. This appears to be a feature of standing on the stage itself. While this is mostly shown during performances, it can also be seen while inspecting or typing a message in chat while standing upon the stage.
    • Walter will use said animations if he is telling a campfire story on the stage. Clicking the campfire repeatedly will cause him to spam the start of the gesture animations.

Stage

Mocking Bird
Miscellaneous
"charlie_heckler"
StageMocking Bird
“It's a setup! It feels too... staged.”
–Wilson
“Setting stuff on fire is so much easier and more entertaining.”
–Willow
“Is like little circus ring.”
–Wolfgang
“The perfect place for a tragedy.”
–Wendy
“THEATER PROTOCOL STATES I MUST BREAK SOMEONE'S LEG”
–WX-78
“The builder must have been inspired by the Greek dramas of antiquity.”
–Wickerbottom
“I don't like being put in the spotlight.”
–Woodie
“Just like the old days.”
–Maxwell
“The stage becköns!”
–Wigfrid
“Oooh! Let's put on a play!”
–Webber
“I have the feeling I am being watched.”
–Warly
“Hello? Somebody there?”
–Wormwood
“Who'd build a stage out here? Oh...”
–Winona
“What a treat, a front-row seat!”
–Wortox
“Hmm, that look important, florp.”
–Wurt
“Are we doing a talent show?”
–Walter
“Honestly, who has time for these theatrics?”
–Wanda
“They're just here to stir up drama.”
–Wilson
“Heh. I kinda like them.”
–Willow
“Wolfgang is thinking about hitting two birds with one stone...”
–Wolfgang
“Their words can't hurt me. Well, maybe just a little...”
–Wendy
“I WILL NOT TAKE CRITICISM FROM ORGANICS”
–WX-78
“I wouldn't classify their criticism as constructive.”
–Wickerbottom
“Birds... it's always the birds...”
–Woodie
“Tough crowd.”
–Maxwell
“Begöne, critics!”
–Wigfrid
“We don't like those birds very much.”
–Webber
“What a rude bird!”
–Warly
“Mean tweeters, hurt friends' feelings!”
–Wormwood
“You can't please everyone.”
–Winona
“Hyuyu, what a funny pair!”
–Wortox
“Hey! No make fun of scale-less, that job mine!”
–Wurt
“What mean birds! At least I think they're birds.”
–Walter
“I don't remember asking for their opinion.”
–Wanda
“A gift graciously bestowed to you by the Queen of the Constant, to enrich your sad, futile little lives. She was even kind enough to prepare costumes and a script!”
-
The Stage is a naturally generating Structure in Don't Starve Together. It always spawns in a Set Piece consisting of tiles of Lush Carpet and Mosaic Flooring, with a Lectern and two Prickly Stagehands beside it. Players can interact with the Stage to begin the play, but they must have the correct Costumes and number of characters. Examining the Lectern shows the required characters for the next act.
If there isn't enough players to fulfill the needs for the entire cast, Mannequins wearing the costumes can substitute for them. Excess Mannequins are ignored, and they must be built on the Stage to count as performers: the Set Piece comes with a single Mannequin off-stage, which holds a complete Doll costume to start the players off.

Year of the Clockwork Knight[edit | edit source]
Only during the Event: Year of the Clockwork Knight
During the event Year of the Clockwork Knight, some of the stage structures will look different.
Also after the player makes an offer to the Clockwork Knight Shrine, coming close to the Stage will spawn 2 Briar Wolves that drop Red Pouch, a Briar Wolf that drops Princess Mask, a Briar Wolf that drops Princess Costume and, if the player does not have My Knights Four and I, a Briar Wolf that drops My Knights Four and I.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Wilson quotes the song "Major-General's Song" from Gilbert and Sullivan's 1879 comic opera The Pirates of Penzance.
Willow quotes her own modified version of The Tyger by William Blake.
Wendy quotes the 1819 poem "La Belle Dame sans Merci" (or in English, "the Beautiful Lady Without Mercy") by John Keats, with a title derived from an earlier 15th century work by Alain Chartier, whose works are alluded to in the 1819 poem.
Woodie's soliloquy will be cut short after just the first line if Lucy is not in his inventory, regardless if she's on-stage with him.
Her text is visible to survivors other than the Woodie holding her as she "performs".
Maxwell's commentary in the end of the Act 2 Scene 1 was "Is that really how she remembers it?".
Wigfrid sings instead of having a soliloquy, making her the only character with in-game voice acting (as opposed to an instrument).
Despite this, her soliloquy is ironically the only one to use zero actual words.
Webber quotes either the nursery rhyme "Little Miss Muffet" or the poem "The Spider and the Fly" at random.
This also makes him the only character with multiple, wholly distinct soliloquys to himself.
Warly quotes "Le chant de ceux qui s'en vont sur mer" (or in English The Seaman's Song) by Victor Hugo, circa 1868.
Wortox quotes A Midsummer Night's Dream, specifically Act 2 Scene 1.
Wanda quotes the poem "You Are Old, Father William" from Alice's Adventures in Wonderland.
In a Refresh Rhymes With Play Dev Cast about "A Little Drama Update", May Wa came in with homemade Mocking Birds puppets. Later, she posted via Instagram instructions on how to create such puppets.
While standing on the stage, the player has a unique talking animation, gesturing about as they talk. This appears to be a feature of standing on the stage itself. While this is mostly shown during performances, it can also be seen while inspecting or typing a message in chat while standing upon the stage.
Walter will use said animations if he is telling a campfire story on the stage. Clicking the campfire repeatedly will cause him to spam the start of the gesture animations.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Sân khấu là một Cấu trúc được tạo ra một cách tự nhiên trong Don't Starve Together. Nó luôn xuất hiện trong Bộ đồ bao gồm các ô Thảm tươi tốt và Sàn khảm, với một Bục giảng và hai Nhân viên sân khấu có gai bên cạnh. Set Piece thường xuất hiện trong quần xã Triple MacTusk, mặc dù nó cũng có thể xuất hiện ở nơi khác.
title: Stage
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Object Infobox |name = Stage |image = Stage.png |spawnCode = "charlie_stage"
"charlie_seat"}} {{Object Infobox |name = Mocking Bird |image = Mocking Bird.png |spawnCode = "charlie_heckler"}} |-|Stage= {{Quotes |wilson = It's a setup! It feels too... staged. |willow = Setting stuff on fire is so much easier and more entertaining. |wolfgang = Is like little circus ring. |wendy = The perfect place for a tragedy. |wx78 = THEATER PROTOCOL STATES I MUST BREAK SOMEONE'S LEG |wickerbottom = The builder must have been inspired by the Greek dramas of antiquity. |woodie = I don't like being put in the spotlight. |maxwell = Just like the old days. |wigfrid = The stage becköns! |webber = Oooh! Let's put on a play! |warly = I have the feeling I am being watched. |wormwood = Hello? Somebody there? |winona = Who'd build a stage out here? Oh... |wortox = What a treat, a front-row seat! |wurt = Hmm, that look important, florp. |walter = Are we doing a talent show? |wanda = Honestly, who has time for these theatrics? |choose = maxwell}} |-|Mocking Bird= {{Quotes |walter=What mean birds! At least I think they're birds. |wanda=I don't remember asking for their opinion. |warly=What a rude bird! |wigfrid=Begöne, critics! |maxwell=Tough crowd. |webber=We don't like those birds very much. |wendy=Their words can't hurt me. Well, maybe just a little... |wickerbottom=I wouldn't classify their criticism as constructive. |willow=Heh. I kinda like them. |wilson=They're just here to stir up drama. |winona=You can't please everyone. |wolfgang=Wolfgang is thinking about hitting two birds with one stone... |woodie=Birds... it's always the birds... |wormwood=Mean tweeters, hurt friends' feelings! |wortox=Hyuyu, what a funny pair! |wurt=Hey! No make fun of scale-less, that job mine! |wx78=I WILL NOT TAKE CRITICISM FROM ORGANICS |choose=wendy }} {{Scrapbook Info|A gift graciously bestowed to you by the Queen of the Constant, to enrich your sad, futile little lives. She was even kind enough to prepare costumes and a script!}} The '''Stage''' is a naturally generating [[Structure]] in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. It always spawns in a [[Set Piece]] consisting of tiles of [[Lush Carpet]] and [[Mosaic Flooring]], with a Lectern and two [[Prickly Stagehand]]s beside it. The Set Piece often spawns in [[MacTusk Plains|Triple MacTusk]] biome, although it can also spawn elsewhere. The Stage can be used to act out a few plays. The player can insert a [[Playbills|Playbill]] into the Lectern to change the play. One of the plays is written by [[Charlie]] and serves as a metaphor of her life and how she came to rule over [[The Constant]] from the [[Nightmare Throne]]. Players can interact with the Stage to begin the play, but they must have the correct [[Costumes]] and number of characters. Examining the Lectern shows the required characters for the next act. If there isn't enough players to fulfill the needs for the entire cast, [[Mannequin]]s wearing the costumes can substitute for them. Excess Mannequins are ignored, and they must be built on the Stage to count as performers: the Set Piece comes with a single Mannequin off-stage, which holds a complete Doll costume to start the players off. {{TocLimit|2}}{{Clear}} =={{Pic|32|Gramophone}} The Plays== {{Object Infobox |name = Lectern |image = Playbill.png|The Enchanted Doll Lectern Void.png|The Pall Lectern Ancient.png|A Task Complete Lectern Princess.png|My Knights Four and I |spawnCode = "charlie_lectern" |perk = When examining, it tells the current scene and act along with the costumes required for it.}} When a play begins, '''Mocking Birds''' will appear, perching on top of the Stage to spectate and provide commentary, heckling the actors in the process. Shadow hands will possess the involved actors and have them act out the scenes. If the casting is incorrect or the script is interrupted, the performance fails, spawning 3-6 [[Briar Wolf|Briar Wolves]] and closing the Stage for 3 – 6 days. Players can also perform a soliloquy if they are the sole actor, with unique soliloquies for both the characters in the play and the survivors themselves. There is also an epilogue scene featuring the Blacksmith and Queen. {{clear}} ===Poor Performance=== {{scroll box|content= ===Bad costumes=== Mocking Birds * "Something's wrong!", * "Check your costumes!", * "We'll come back.", * "When you get it right!", ===Repeat costumes=== Mocking Birds * "Are you kidding?" * "Am I seeing double?!" * "No understudies!" ===No script=== Mocking Birds * "Something's off." * "They're going off-script!" * "I didn't come for improv!" * "We're out." }} ===Character Specific=== {{scroll box|content= ===Wilson soliloquy {{Pic32|Wilson Filter}}=== * "I am the very model of a modern Major-General" * "I've information vegetable, animal, and mineral" * "I'm very well acquainted, too, with matters Mathematical" * "I understand equations, both the simple and quadratical" ===Willow soliloquy {{Pic32|Willow Filter}}=== * "Fire fire, burning bright," * "In the forests of the night;" * "And in the mountains, and the plains," * "And the towns, and the cities," * "Everywhere! Burn it all down!! Hahaha!!" * (Willow performs the "/happy" emote.) * "Yeah, pretty sure that's how that poem goes." ===Wolfgang soliloquy {{Pic32|Wolfgang Filter}}=== * "Wolfgang is not just man of big muscles," * "Is man of mighty feelings too." * "Will read poem to you now:" * "Wolfgang likes his friends," * "Though some are weird, make scary magic, and one is spider," * "Will stay with them till very end," * "Make Wolfgang's bad day brighter." ===Wendy soliloquy {{Pic32|Wendy Filter}}=== * "O what can ail thee, knight-at-arms," * "So haggard and so woe-begone?" * "The squirrel's granary is full," * "And the harvest's done." * "I see a lily on thy brow," * "With anguish moist and fever-dew," * "And on thy cheeks a fading rose" * "Fast withereth too." ===WX-78 soliloquy {{Pic32|WX-78 Filter}}=== * "PERCEIVE ME, FLESHLINGS" * "YES. GOOD" * "I KNOW YOU MEATBAGS ARE WEAK-WILLED" * "SO AS YOUR OVERLORD" * "I HAVE DECIDED TO ENGAGE IN..." * "UGH..." * "\"MOTIVATIONAL SPEAKING\"" * "TO INSPIRE YOU TO BE MORE USEFUL TO ME" * "BUT SINCE YOU HAVE SAD LITTLE MEAT BRAINS" * "I AM KEEPING MY EXPECTATIONS LOW" * "IS THE INSPIRATION WORKING YET?" ===Wickerbottom soliloquy {{Pic32|Wickerbottom Filter}}=== * "Theater as an art form is practiced throughout the world." * "The Western tradition of theater can trace its origins back to ancient Athens;" * "the word "theater" of course coming from the Greek "theatron,"" * "which itself was derived from the verb "theasthai," "to behold."" * "Isn't etymology delightful?" ===Woodie soliloquy {{Pic32|Woodie Filter}}=== Woodie * "Er... I don't really like bein' the center of attention." Lucy (if in inventory) * "Let me try!" * "Chop chop chop, swing swing swing," * "Choppin' is my favourite thing." * "Swing swing swing, chop chop chop," * "Love it way too much to stop!" Woodie * "You've sure got a way with words, Lucy." ===Wes soliloquy {{Pic32|Wes Filter}} (Only exists in game files)=== * "Essayez-vous comprendre mes pensées?" * "Ah, comme c'est gentil à vous!" * "Je suis flatté. :)" Directly translated from french, it reads: * "Are you trying to understand my thoughts?" * "Ah, that's very kind of you!" * "I am flattered. :)" ===Maxwell soliloquy {{Pic32|Maxwell Filter}}=== * "Thank you very much, you are too kind." * "And now, I will pull shadows incarnate from this mysterious tome!" * Maxwell summons two temporary shadow minions that dance next to him before disappearing for his next line. * "The show was better with... well, it was better before." ===Wigfrid soliloquy {{Pic32|Wigfrid Filter}}=== * (Wigfrid sings opera for several seconds.) ===Webber soliloquy ("boy") {{Pic32|Webber Filter}}=== * "Ahem! We're going to say a poem now!" * "Little Miss Muffet she sat on her tuffet," * "eating her curds and whey." * "Along came a spider who sat down beside her" * "And frightened Miss Muffet away!" ===Webber soliloquy ("spider") {{Pic32|Webber Filter}}=== * ""Will you walk into my parlour?"" * "said a spider to a fly;" * "'Tis the prettiest little parlour that ever you did spy." * "The way into my parlour is up a winding stair," * "And I have many pretty things to shew when you are there." * ""Oh no, no!" said the little fly" * "to ask me is in vain" * "For who goes up your winding stair can ne'er come down again." ===Winona soliloquy {{Pic32|Winona Filter}}=== * "There once was a gal from the sticks," * "Who went out to look for her sis." * "But she found her too late," * "And got yanked through a gate," * "Ain't family drama the pits?" ===Warly soliloquy {{Pic32|Warly Filter}}=== * "Adieu, patrie!" * "L'onde est en furie." * "Adieu, patrie," * "Azur!" * "Adieu, maison, treille au fruit mûr," * "Adieu, les fleurs d'or du vieux mur!" ===Wortox soliloquy {{Pic32|Wortox Filter}}=== * "The wisest aunt, telling the saddest tale," * "Sometime for three-foot stool mistaketh me;" * "Then slip I from her bum, down topples she," * "And 'tailor' cries, and falls into a cough;" * "And then the whole quire hold their hips and laugh," * "And waxen in their mirth and neeze and swear" * "A merrier hour was never wasted there." ===Wormwood soliloquy {{Pic32|Wormwood Filter}}=== * "Hello friends!" * "Watching? Hmm..." * "Doo-doo-dee-doop" * "Woo-wah-wah!" * "Deedle-deedle-deedle-woo" * "Wah-wah-wheee!" * "Oop-doodle-doo" * "Bwa-waaaaaaaaaah!" ===Wurt soliloquy {{Pic32|Wurt Filter}}=== * "Glurp... th-this is fairy story. Made by Wurt." * "Once upon time, there was super strong Mermfolk princess." * "Merm princess build houses for all her friends," * "and make pretty decorations from bones of her enemies." * "Everyone live happily ever after, the end." ===Walter soliloquy {{Pic32|Walter Filter}}=== * "I've been teaching Woby lots of tricks, watch!" * "Okay Woby... stay!" * "Good girl!" * "We're uh... we're still working on the rest." * "Still pretty impressive though, right?" ===Wanda soliloquy {{Pic32|Wanda Filter}}=== * ""You are old, Father William," the young man said", * ""And your hair has become very white;", * "And yet you incessantly stand on your head—", * "Do you think, at your age, it is right?"", * ""In my youth," Father William replied to his son,", * ""I feared it might injure the brain;", * "But now that I'm perfectly sure I have none,", * "Why, I do it again and again."", }} ==Year of the Clockwork Knight== {{Event|Year of the Clockwork Knight}} During the event [[Year of the Clockwork Knight]], some of the stage structures will look different. Also after the player makes an offer to the [[Clockwork Knight Shrine]], coming close to the Stage will spawn 2 [[Briar Wolf|Briar Wolves]] that drop [[Lucky Gold Nugget#Clockwork Knight's Red Pouch|Red Pouch]], a Briar Wolf that drops [[Princess Mask]], a Briar Wolf that drops [[Princess Costume]] and, if the player does not have [[My Knights Four and I]], a Briar Wolf that drops [[My Knights Four and I]]. =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * [[Wilson]] quotes the song "[[wikipedia:Major-General%27s_Song|Major-General's Song]]" from [[wikipedia:Gilbert_and_Sullivan|Gilbert and Sullivan]]'s 1879 comic opera ''[[wikipedia:The_Pirates_of_Penzance|The Pirates of Penzance]]''. * [[Willow]] quotes her own modified version of ''[[wikipedia:The_Tyger|The Tyger]]'' by [[wikipedia:William_Blake|William Blake]]. * [[Wendy]] quotes the 1819 poem "''La Belle Dame sans Merci"'' (or in English, "the Beautiful Lady Without Mercy") by [[wikipedia:John_Keats|John Keats]], with a title derived from an earlier 15th century work by [[wikipedia:Alain_Chartier|Alain Chartier]], whose works are alluded to in the 1819 poem. * [[Woodie]]'s soliloquy will be cut short after just the first line if Lucy is not in his inventory, regardless if she's on-stage with him. ** Her text is visible to survivors other than the Woodie holding her as she "performs". * [[Maxwell]]'s commentary in the end of the Act 2 Scene 1 was "Is that really how [[Charlie|she]] remembers it?". * [[Wigfrid]] sings instead of having a soliloquy, making her the only character with in-game voice acting (as opposed to an instrument). ** Despite this, her soliloquy is ironically the only one to use zero actual words. * [[Webber]] quotes either the nursery rhyme "[[wikipedia:Little_Miss_Muffet|Little Miss Muffet]]" or the poem "[[wikipedia:The_Spider_and_the_Fly_(poem)|The Spider and the Fly]]" at random. ** This also makes him the only character with multiple, wholly distinct soliloquys to himself. * [[Warly]] quotes "[https://fr.m.wikisource.org/wiki/Le_Chant_de_ceux_qui_s%E2%80%99en_vont_sur_mer Le chant de ceux qui s'en vont sur mer]" (or in English ''[https://verse.press/poem/the-seamans-song-27724 The Seaman's Song]'') by [[wikipedia:Victor_Hugo|Victor Hugo]], circa 1868. * [[Wortox]] quotes ''[[wikipedia:A_Midsummer_Night%27s_Dream|A Midsummer Night's Dream]]'', specifically Act 2 Scene 1. * [[Wanda]] quotes the poem "[[wikipedia:You_Are_Old,_Father_William|You Are Old, Father William]]" from ''Alice's Adventures in Wonderland''. *In a Refresh ''Rhymes With Play'' Dev Cast about "A Little Drama Update", May Wa came in with homemade Mocking Birds puppets. Later, she posted via Instagram instructions on how to create such puppets. *While standing on the stage, the player has a unique talking animation, gesturing about as they talk. This appears to be a feature of standing on the stage itself. While this is mostly shown during performances, it can also be seen while inspecting or typing a message in chat while standing upon the stage. **[[Walter]] will use said animations if he is telling a campfire story on the stage. Clicking the campfire repeatedly will cause him to [https://youtu.be/seyEy-nn5p8?t=38 spam the start of the gesture animations]. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Stage (Year of the Clockwork Knight).png|Stage (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Stagehand (Year of the Clockwork Knight).png|Stagehand (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight).png|Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Void.png|Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Ancient.png|Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Princess.png|Lectern (Year of the Clockwork Knight) Mocking Bird (Year of the Clockwork Knight).png|Mocking Bird (Year of the Clockwork Knight) {{clear}} =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== RWP ALD Stage concept art1.png|Stage concept art from Rhymes With Play stream.
